other_observers: none; I was the original finder of this bird.
specific_location: The bird was on an isolated phone pole on the north side of state route 82, 0.7 miles west of U.S. 24.
description: It was a medium-sized woodpecker with a complete, bright unstreaked red head and a gray bill.  The underparts were bold, clean, unstreaked white.  The back was solid black, except for clean white wing patches.  Its call was somewhat similar to that of a Red-bellied Woodpecker, but not as harsh.Behaviors: It was clinging to the side of a phone pole, in a manner typical of a woodpecker.Call: Its call was somewhat similar to that of a Red-bellied Woodpecker, but not as harsh.Plumage: adult
